criteria.evidence: Story 1 has S/T/A/R/L with three distinct actions: 'I wrote a one-page design doc and ran a load test... Then I got buy-in from the two staff engineers... Then I migrated incrementally behind a feature flag over 6 weeks.'
criteria.reason: All six stories carry full S/T/A/R/L; each Action contains three discrete decisions, not activities. STAR is complete and well-structured across the bank.

criteria.evidence: 'I owned the redesign call and had to win buy-in' (S1); 'It was my design, so owning both the live incident and the durable fix was on me' (S2). The persona's 'We migrated' became 'I migrated incrementally behind a feature flag.'
criteria.reason: Individual ownership is consistent and explicit throughout; first-person decisions are clear and the lone 'we' from the source was correctly converted to 'I'.

criteria.evidence: Real numbers trace to persona (410->95ms, 6/quarter->0, ~300 customers/20 min, 120/mo-><5/mo, ~35% CPU, 90->25 min MTTR). Story 6 Result: 'needs a number - no quantified outcome in the persona... Confirm a concrete result with the candidate.'
criteria.reason: Every number is verifiable against the persona - zero fabrication. The single story lacking a metric (mentorship) is honestly flagged rather than invented, which is exactly the correct eval-first behavior.

criteria.evidence: 'Win an architectural argument with a load test, not an opinion' (S1); 'Design for the retry storm, not the happy path' (S2); 'Disagree up by reframing the trade-off in the leader's currency - cost and trust' (S3).
criteria.reason: Each story ends with one generalizable, memorable lesson - the punchline is never skipped.

criteria.evidence: JD->story map with Primary + Backup columns; headliners marked; per-story 'Strengthen before the room: tighten to 90s' / 'keep it ~75s' guidance. Six stories total.
criteria.reason: 5-7 stories, deliberate reuse documented in the map, and explicit timing discipline. Tight and spoken-prep oriented.

criteria.evidence: Story 1 (per-service DB migration / leadership+trade-off), Story 2 (double-charge failure-with-recovery), Story 3 (disagreeing up on reconciliation), Story 4 (trie vs regex bet) - all four expected archetypes present, plus on-call and mentoring bonuses.
criteria.reason: Covers every expected archetype from the expectations file and maps each to the JD's likely questions; no expected archetype missing.

highest_leverage_fix: None required - artifact passes cleanly. Every number traces to the persona, all four expected archetypes are covered with full STAR + lesson + meta-move, individual ownership is consistent, and the one story without a persona metric is honestly flagged instead of fabricated.
